Beschrieb: The platform collects offerings for farm holidays. More than 30 years ago Hans Wespi started  "Ferien auf dem Bauernhof" with only four farms. Today, the association counts 100 farms and all farms meet the quality seal criteria for farm holidays. The president Rita Barth leads the association and has the goal to market the offers on the homepage.

The Swiss Tourism Quality Label has been successfully applied for 20 years. The Research Institute for Leisure and Tourism and the Frey Academy developed in cooperation the training and certification system, which aims to improve the quality in tourism enterprises. The quality program distinguishes businesses that want to improve their quality continuously and are prepared to give their best for the guest.

Veloland Switzerland was opened in 1998 and the foundation was transformed into the SwitzerlandMobility Foundation in 2008. The initiative is supported by the federal government, cantons, tourism, transport and sport. The aim of the foundation is to make cycling pleasant and to show cyclists the most beautiful routes in Switzerland. Cycling in Switzerland offers a selection of attractive routes, clear signposting and tourist services.

The "Swiss Alps 3000" association is actively committed to emission-free mobility in the Alpine region. To this end, initiator Ulo Gertsch had planned to convert a snow groomer into a hydrogen-powered piste vehicle. The reason for this was that the piste groomers are used in a very sensitive environment and emit pollutants. This idea was to be implemented in cooperation with mountain railway companies, hydroelectric power industry, vehicle industry and H2 research.
Akteurinnen: Educational and research institutions, Clubs, Companies

Beschrieb: The four partners Gstaad Saanenland Tourism, Golden Pass Services, Bergbahnen Saanenland-Simmental AG and Swiss Post jointly founded the Saanenmöser Service Centre. Thanks to this cooperation, the post office was able to reopen and the station was staffed again. The project provided Saanenmöser with a functioning public service offering without generating significant additional costs for the individual partners.

Helga and Paul Wyler founded kuhleasing.ch over 35 years ago. Today, their daughter Iris has taken over the business and leases a cow to customers for a summer. The customers receive between 70-120 kg of cheese per cow and work one day on the alp. The aim is to give people an understanding of life on the alp and thus strengthen the understanding between the urban and the mountain population. It shows the importance of mountain farming and thus ensures its preservation for future generations.
Themenfelder: Alternative production & consumption, Agriculture, Alpine farming, Local products

Beschrieb: The organisation ViaStoria, which is a foundation for transport history, has launched the project Cultural Routes Switzerland. On twelve main and 300 regional routes, it provides access to transportation history and sights in Switzerland. Cultural Routes Switzerland promotes the preservation and sustainable development of the natural and cultural landscape and links offers from tourism, agriculture, non-motorized traffic and education. Linking protection and use objectives enables new experiences and regional value creation.

Local jurisdictions, in cooperation with tourism businesses, provide exclusive external marriage locations as wedding venues in the Bernese Oberland. These include locations such as Schadau Castle, Oberhofen, Spiez or the Grandhotel Giessbach. The aim is to satisfy a growing social need and to promote cooperation with tourism businesses and local companies.

Since 2005, the tourism destinations Interlaken Tourism and Jungfrau Region Tourism have been awarding this annual prize together with the regional development authority. The prize is awarded to the managers of particularly valuable cultural landscapes in the eastern Bernese Oberland. The aim is to highlight the importance of agriculture and its maintenance to the population and thus raise awareness. The work of the farmers should thus be more highly valued.

Together with ELVIA Reiseversicherung, Lenk-Simmental Tourism has developed an insurance package in which one can insure your holiday against fog. If one travels to Lenk and finds him/herself in the fog, the price of the ski pass or hotel room will be refunded. The aim is to offer a guest-friendly compensation and the manager of Lenk-Simmental Tourism is sure that  insurance benefits will be claimed very rarely.

The MiniMuseumMürren association was founded in 2008 to enable visitors and residents to learn about the history of Mürren. The aim of the association is to enhance the public space by reviving shop windows and to create a link to the present. New exhibitions are placed in the shop windows every year, thus creating a connection with already existing local spaces.

Titel: Schnuppercamp für die Hotellerie und Gastronomie
Beschrieb: The taster camp gives young people insights into the world of the hotel, catering and tourism industry. The current activities in the Valais and Bernese Oberland are supported by HotellerieSuisse, the respective cantonal regional associations of HotellerieSuisse and the cantons. Companies in mountain and tourism regions sometimes have difficulty finding apprentices. Participating young people from all over Switzerland not only have the opportunity to get to know the professions in the industry, but they also experience how attractive it is to live and work in a tourism region.

Hotelleriesuisse is responsible for the hotelpower.ch project, which was developed in 2003 by various players in the hotel industry. Due to rising energy prices and climate change, the project aims to save energy. It aims to sensitize the industry to energy efficiency measures and thus reduce CO2 emissions and costs. As many companies from the hotel industry as possible are to be included in the target agreements.
Akteurinnen: Educational and research institutions, Companies, Associations

Beschrieb: The foundation was founded in 2008 by hotelleriesuisse and private actors. An archive was set up to collect and safeguard the origins of hotel and tourism history. The objects should be available to the public and serve as a source for research and education. Hotelarchiv thinks that it is important to understand history in order to shape the future. Thus, hotel and tourism history helps shape innovations for the future.

The ViaPostaAlpina route was founded in collaboration between Kulturwege Schweiz and PostBus AG. It is a cultural trail through the Swiss Alps over the Grimsel, Nufenen, Gotthard and Susten passes. As the landscape passes by, historical insights into the development of the postal bus are provided during the journey. The bus journey is combined with hikes along historic paths and can be completed in six daily stages.

Since 2005, Bus alpin has been active in regions where there are no or only poor public transport connections. However, many well-known destinations are located exactly in such regions. These destinations often suffer from the increasing motorized traffic. Bus alpin has the goal to expand the public transport services to such places and supports already 17 regions. Partners of the association are PostBus, the Swiss Parks Network and many more.

In 2013, the project "Holidays - accessible for all" was launched as a collaboration between Swiss Youth Hostels and the Denk an mich foundation. The aim was to make the Swiss youth hostels' services barrier-free, from their Internet presence to their homes. In doing so, they wanted to send a signal to the tourism industry and draw attention to the issue of accessibility. It should be possible to copy and imitate the project.

Swiss Tavolata was created after the success of the TV show "Landfrauenküche" (countrywomen's cuisine) and enables people to taste traditional Swiss cuisine at home with farmers and countrywomen. After a tour of the farm, a dish is prepared using local products, at least 80% of which are from the farm itself. The aim of Swiss Tavolata is to combine agriculture and tourism and thus provide an additional income for the farmers.

The municipality of Adelboden and Adelboden Tourism have jointly launched the "Renovation is half the battle" project. The project creates incentives for second home owners to renovate their aging properties. The municipality is offering 20 hours of consultancy services for the refurbishment, on condition that local businesses are taken into account during construction and that the apartment is subsequently rented out for at least 3 years. The aim is to increase the number of overnight stays, to support local business and thus also to increase the tax income of the municipality.
Themenfelder: Tourism, Construction and timber industry, Accommodation

Beschrieb: Under the name "Made in Bern" the BE! Tourismus AG 2012 was founded to create a self-confident marketing presence for the Tourism Canton of Bern. The Canton of Berne and the tourist destinations of Berne founded the AG with the long-term goal of increasing the added value of tourism and becoming the No. 1 in tourism in Switzerland. This is to be achieved in part by increasing the visibility of tourism and strengthening the exchange between partners.

The Flatnap pilot project was founded in 2017 as a start-up by Dominik and Daniel Stocker. Flatnap is a subscription for hotel accommodation. For an all-inclusive price, short trips of one night can be booked as often as you like via the platform. Additional services or additional nights can then be booked with the respective hotel. Flatnap started in Graubünden and now also has a host in Grindelwald. The two founders have the vision of becoming the No. 1 for short holidays in Switzerland.

In 2011 the Hotelier Association Gstaad Saanenland founded the  YourGstaad. They have recognized the challenge of the shortage of skilled workers and want to make Gstaad better known as a place to work. YourGstaad offers employees a platform on which they can find current job offers, discounts, further training or accommodation. In this way, the employees are to be shown the attractiveness of the destination and the hotel companies benefit jointly.

In 2015, 13 hotels joined forces to create the Frutigland Hotel Cooperation. In the Frutigland area, there are many small hotel businesses which are facing great financial challenges. The aim of the cooperation is to promote better cooperation between the companies so that they can help each other. However, in order to establish a cooperation, the businesses must realize that they can benefit from each other and not think of losing guests to other cooperation partners.

In 2016 the  Swiss Ranger Station Haslital was founded by Franziska and Thomas Herren. They were of the opinion that one must experience and understand nature in order to love and protect it. The two created a place in the Haslital for people who like to be in nature. The idea was that people could spend their days in a wagon loft and be on the road with the ranger. Unfortunately, no permission was given for the wagon loft and the project was abandoned.
Themenfelder: Tourism, Leisure activity, Accommodation, Ecology, Education

Titel:
Schnupper- und Lehrstellenbörse für die Hotellerie und 
Gastronomie
Beschrieb: HotellerieSuisse and GastroSuisse launched the taster and apprenticeship exchange for the hotel and restaurant industry. The challenge of finding sufficient young talent in these sectors was evident. This is how berufehotelgastro.ch was launched. It is intended to provide young people with a platform for the professions in the industry. On the one hand, apprenticeships or trial positions are offered and on the other hand, the profession is presented in more detail. Furthermore, there are many other useful functions for young people.

This project was launched in 2017 by CIPRA as project sponsor and other partners. The aim was to find and develop innovative ideas for sustainable tourism in the Swiss Alpine region. The Innovation Generator offers the project initiators technical and financial support and helps to network the various players. Through co-creation, innovative ideas are to be created and further developed.
Akteurinnen: Educational and research institutions, Clubs, Tourism organisations, Companies, Associations

Beschrieb: Several tourist destinations wanted to work out how the Tourist Office will change in the age of digitalization and how they can still remain attractive. Therefore the Tourist Office 3.0 project was started by the company gutundgut GmbH. The project was based on an analysis of the needs of both guests and service providers. For this purpose, the guests and service providers were systematically interviewed and a direct exchange between the destinations took place.
Akteurinnen: Educational and research institutions, Tourism organisations, Companies

Beschrieb: In 2005, the foundation was founded by the Swiss Heritage Society. The foundation is at the interface between tourism and heritage conservation and is committed to the preservation of architectural monuments. However, such monuments are often located in remote areas. The foundation wants to upgrade them so that new added value can be created for these places. As soon as guests move into the buildings, regional shops will also benefit.
Akteurinnen: Educational and research institutions, Companies, Tourism organisations, Foundations
Themenfelder: Tourism, Construction and timber industry, Accommodation, Culture

Beschrieb: BLS and Gstaad Mountain Rides jointly developed the White Bull in 2008. It was a train that takes guests from Berne via Wabern, Belp, Thun and Spiez to Zweisimmen/Gstaad. The aim was to create a congestion-free and ecological alternative to directly get to the top of ski stations. The White Bull operated on weekends and on public holidays to relieve the normal train operation. The train was aimed at young people and designed in such a way that even the journey to the station was an experience. Ski passes could be purchased directly on the train.

The "Götti System" project was built by the Bernese mountain railways in 2007. The aim behind it is that larger Bernese ski resorts should help the smaller resorts and support them with their knowledge. For the larger ski areas, the existence of the smaller ones is extremely important, as many people learn to ski at the pre-alpine ski lifts. If these were to disappear, there would be even fewer people practicing winter sports. So young and old help each other.

In 2005, seven farmers in the Diemtigtal recognized that more and more cheese dairies were disappearing and that it was becoming increasingly difficult for them too. Therefore they joined to form the Kiley-Alpen cooperative and together they continued to run the Kiley alpine cheese dairy. With the purchase they were able to stop the disappearance of farms and secure their existence. The cooperative operates today's largest organic alpine cheese dairy and the organic status contributes greatly to its success.
Themenfelder: Agriculture, Alpine farming, Local products, Ecology

Beschrieb: In 1996 the Brog family received three black-nosed sheep as a gift. More and more animals followed and today the family owns about 200 sheep. The Wollreich Haslital farm and the product range expanded considerably. Innovations such as sheep's wool balls for pillows and nursing rings as well as duvets and bed pads filled with their own sheep's wool led to success throughout Switzerland. Twice a year the Wollreich collects wool for Swisswool, which processes it as filling material for outdoor jackets or as insulation.
Themenfelder: Tourism, Agriculture, Leisure activity, Industry, Local products

Beschrieb: The education departments of the Cantons of Geneva and Bern, together with Gstaad Saanenland Tourism, founded "Deux im Schnee". This is a bilingual snow sports camp in the destination of Gstaad with young people. In this way, the ski region contributes to the promotion of young people in snow sports, cultural diversity is supported and the young people can improve their language skills with a lot of fun.

In Innertkirchen, the project to convert the rock cavern into a cheese maturing warehouse was completed in 2014. This allowed an already existing, unused infrastructure to be converted and reused. A tasting room is also to be created in the gallery. The tenant not only stores his cheese, but also has the obligation to store the cheese of the alpine cooperatives.

The community garden Aarefeld was founded in 2012 under the sponsorship of the associations "Ärdele" and "Zukunft Wohnen" in the Thun region in order to organize a garden meeting together. The aim is to create a meeting place that can be used in various ways. Vegetables and fruits for the use of the association will be planted on the area and passed on to third parties.

In 2013 the cooperative was founded by eight members. With the perspective that people want to live in harmony with each other, the multi-generation project was created, in which different generations lives together in a former hotel. The project aims to treat each other with respect and tries to build a benevolent community.

In 2017 the association was founded so that together an interim solution can be found for the unused Hotel Touriste. The hotel is situated centrally in Interlaken. The association has rebuilt the premises under the new name Blago Bung. The place is now a cultural meeting point, event location and a project for young people from the region.
Themenfelder: Alternative production & consumption, Community development, Culture

Beschrieb: This solidarity-based agriculture project was founded by the small company Biogemüse Erlengut in 2017. The aim is to build up a food production in which producers and consumers organize themselves together. In this way it can be planned how much vegetables are needed and surpluses can be avoided. The vegetables also do not have to meet market standards and long transport routes are eliminated.
Themenfelder: Alternative production & consumption, Agriculture, Local products, Ecology

Beschrieb: The cooperative SpiezSolar was founded in 1999 with the construction of the first photovoltaic system on a school building in Spiez. Further projects followed. SpiezSolar is primarily responsible for providing advice and information for private individuals and businesses. The cooperative stands for environmentally friendly procurement, distribution and promotion of renewable energies. Meanwhile there are more than 300 solar plants in Spiez, which have been supported by SpiezSolar.

Pia and Niklaus Hirschi came to the "Louberli" in 2013 and founded a place for animals of mercy on the mountain farm. The animals are allowed to live far away from factory farming and do not have to produce any more. The family also offers holidays on the Gnadenhof in the mountain house. Since 2015, they have had 15 decoy posts on the farm, on which information is written and with which they draw attention to their agricultural work.

David Perreten and Thomas Schläppi jointly launched this cheese dairy in 2014. They decided to do so because their organic milk was not processed in the region and this did not fit into their philosophy. Their aim is to keep the added value in the region and to aim for direct marketing. The two farmers rely on extensive dairy farming and closed cycles on their farms.

In 1996, Andreas Jutzeler had the idea to develop an all-terrain wheelchair to enable people with mobility impairments to enjoy leisure activities in nature. Mountain Drive was presented in 2016 and since then, four such wheelchairs are in use on the Stockhorn, where the wheelchairs allow to drive around the Hinterstockensee. Mountain Drive is now available in various other destinations.

In many places, small shops have closed down and as a result, the range of regional products on offer has become increasingly limited. With the opening of the Eigerness shop, these regional products will be marketed. For this purpose 120 producers are members of the association. The shop is run according to the "Eigerness Philosophy" (personal / sustainable / connecting) and covers all areas of "Eigerness" (culinary, design & handicraft, lifestyle and outdoor).

The "Digital Roadmap" project was launched in Biel-Seeland in 2016 and also reached the Bernese Oberland in 2018. The aim is to support companies in the Bernese Oberland in their digital transformation. In the Digital Roadmap, a digital business model is developed in a joint process. To this end, the companies are supported from determining the current situation and developing ideas, to concretizing them and implementing them.

SimmeSaane was launched in 1997 in four regions of the Bernese Oberland. It was a 300-kilometre-long network of cycle paths through these regions. The aim was to strengthen environmentally conscious tourism and to stimulate economic activity in the regions. To this end, regional products and services were brought in and networked. The cooperation between the various sectors was intended to attract new guests.

The association spediBEO was founded in 2002 and is a platform for the development of logistics projects. The aim is to discuss current problems across sectors and thus to coordinate regional needs. The project should be self-supporting and supply the peripheral areas in the Bernese Oberland with goods. Through better coordination among the various players, empty runs can be reduced.

The company Tropenhaus Frutigen AG was founded in 2003 and the house was opened in 2009. It plays a leading role in the use of renewable energies in the Alpine region. For this purpose, hot water from the Lötschberg base tunnel is used. It is a unique combination of exotic flora, aquaculture and knowledge transfer in an Alpine environment. Wherever possible, attention is paid to closed and resource-saving cycles.
Themenfelder: Tourism, Leisure activity, Local products, Gastronomy, Ecology

Beschrieb: The "Zukunft Hasliberg" project was launched in 2015 and the reason for this was the impact of the second home initiative, which limits the number of new second homes in mountain regions in Switzerland, on the construction industry in the community. Through the project, the population and those affected were to be able to co-determine the future of Hasliberg together, thus building on the strengths and potential. The main goal was to increase the activity of the community as a place to live and as a tourist destination and to this end an authentic village image was to be created.
Akteurinnen: Education and research institutions, Public sector
Themenfelder: Tourism, Agriculture, Community development, Accommodation, Gastronomy, Housing, Mobility, Alpine economy, Construction & timber industry

Beschrieb: In 2006, Axpo AG founded a heating network together with a civic community, the Burgergemeinde Wimmis. As the financial resources came from Axpo AG, it was a challenge for the Burgergemeinde to participate in decisions. From 2011 onwards, the Burgergemeinde and the municipality tried to take over the heating network. Since the takeover in 2016, 20 new contracts have already been signed and the plant optimized.

In 2017, the KAFOL project was launched and it coordinates the asylum and refugee system in the Bernese Oberland. The aim is to enable and improve the professional integration of asylum seekers, temporarily admitted persons and refugees. With the earliest possible experience in the world of work, it is possible to find employment and no longer live on social welfare. To this end, there is cooperation among the organizations working in the field of asylum and refugees.

The AlpenLernen project was launched in 2014 to give school classes the opportunity to experience the high mountain landscape of the UNESCO World Heritage Site Jungfrau-Aletsch, and thereby to better understand the principle of sustainable development of the Alpine region. The SAC and UNESCO have jointly launched this project, and school classes can experience a project work in the Alps over five days. The aim is also to arouse interest in sports activities in the Alpine region. The Alpine Learning Project Week is now being held for the first time in other areas of Switzerland.

At the end of 2018, five golf clubs decided to enter into the "Private Partner Golf (PPG)" partnership and a sixth club joined in 2019. The clubs have realized that it is no longer the golfers` expectations to always play in one place. This means that golfers can only be members of one golf club and can play on all six courses for a minimal increase in annual fees.

The Foundation Retungsdienst Frutigland was established in 2000, when it was proposed to reduce the 16 rescue services in the canton of Berne to four. The fear was that the long distances between the new bases would lead to a deterioration in emergency care. This suggestion was averted and the Frutigen Hospital set up a rescue service. The aim of the foundation is to promote the training and further education of paramedics and other personnel and thus maintain a rescue service in Fruttigland.

HolzNetzWerk Brünig was launched in 2016 as a result of "Zukunft Hasliberg". The aim was to find a solution for networking regional companies from the timber industry. A symposium helped to identify synergies and clarify local needs. This symposium should always be held in Brünig, as it is a basis for networking local players. The HolzNetzWerk Brünig became the Forum Holz & Wirtschaft Schweiz.

For two years, the Lucerne University of Applied Sciences and Arts worked together on the "Future Hasliberg" project. This gave rise to the idea of "Generationenwohnen Hasliberg" (multi-generational housing project). The aim of the project is to create needs-based forms of housing and living for older people in Hasliberg. This requires a suitable form of living in a central location. During this time, a working group consisting mainly of the elderly population from Hasliberg has laid the foundations for a future generation house through voluntary work.

The BEO HOLZ joint venture was founded in 1968. It is an association of forest owners' associations, sawmills, master carpenters, master joiners and wood energy, which BEO HOLZ jointly operates and promotes. The common goal is the promotion of wood in the Bernese Oberland. They are committed to the use of wood as an energy source and building material in the BeO. Furthermore, the interest in wood as a value-added material is promoted and represented in politics.

Titel: Qualifizierungsplattform Regionen Berner Oberland und Oberwallis
Beschrieb: The Bernese Oberland is the responsible body for the project "Qualification Platform Regions Upper Valais and Bernese Oberland", which was approved in 2015. The project is being implemented together with the Upper Valais region. It aims to increase the qualification of employees and to strengthen the adaptability of employers in the regions. For this reason, a qualification platform was established with the three fields of action: Further training, networks and innovation and technology transfer.

In 2015, the obstetrics department at the Zweisimmen hospital was closed and basic obstetric care in the Saaneland region was not satisfactory. For this reason, a cooperative was founded in 2015 by 49 interested parties in order to provide basic obstetric care close to their homes. Finally, in 2017, the Maternité Alpine birthplace in Zweisimmen was put into operation. The cooperative now has over 340 members. The cooperation between practicing doctors, midwives, Spitex services, etc. is strengthened and maintained.

In 2011, the solar ship Cassiopeia set sail for the first time on Lake Thun. The Charrois family owns the ship and did most of the work themselves. The ship was developed for the public transport of 12 people and a solar roof is used to produce electricity for the electric motor. With this idea, the family wants to draw attention to the potential of solar energy and demonstrate sustainable leisure mobility. Since 2014, various educational trips have been carried out in cooperation with Aqua Viva.
Themenfelder: Tourism, Leisure activity, Mobility, Ecology, Energy

Beschrieb: In 2016, the Claire & George Foundation together with hotelleriesussie launched the Hotel Accessibility Project. Other tourism and disability organisations also participated in the project. It is a hotel platform with information on accessibility of accommodation. After the test phase, it was planned that the information would be stored in the hotelleriesuisse database. During the project phase, the hotels were checked on site by a team regarding accessibility.

The private office rundum mobil GmbH is committed to efficient and social mobility. In the year 2000, various courses for senior women were launched. These courses deal with questions such as: How can I buy a ticket from a ticket machine, how can I travel safely on public transport, etc.? The pilot project was launched in the Thun region, working together with various players such as the SBB, Postauto and the Bern cantonal police.

The Strandhotel Seeblick Faulensee wants to use the heating potential of lakes and rivers together with the SpiezSolar cooperative. In a pilot project, several properties are to be heated with lake water. This method of lake heating is already being used at many other locations in Switzerland. For this purpose, water is pumped out of the lake, even in cold seasons, the heat is extracted and fed back into the lake at a slightly cooler temperature.

In 2018 the project "Integrated Health Network Simmental-Saanenland" was presented. It is intended to ensure that basic and hospital care in the Simmental-Saanenland region is guaranteed in the long term. To this end, a health campus in Zweisimmen and a health center in Saanen are being considered. Health care in the region is to be ensured by an integrated health network with a health campus in Zweisimmen and an integrated health centet in Saanen. This means that there is a strong network between the healthcare partners.

In order for the canton to co-finance the primary school in Guttannen, the school lacked pupils. Therefore, a working group was set up in 2015 to look into the future of the school. In 2017, the village voted to approve the reorganization of the primary school. As a solution, the Bernese private school NMS took over the Guttannen primary school as an external location.

The International Commission for the Protection of the Alps CIPRA has launched the "Youth Alpine Interrail" in 2019. Eight Alpine countries could be explored in a climate-friendly way by train for 50 euros. Young people aged 16-27 could apply for the pass and then share their adventure via social media. At the end, the best photos and stories were awarded. The idea was to show people the Alps and to trigger a more conscious travel to the Alps.

During a tourism conference in 2001, a working group of farmers was formed. Their aim was to market the local products. In the same year the idea of a "Burelädeli" was born, where products of local farmers and artists are sold. In addition, an online Burelädeli was developed, in which a gift basket can be selected.

The self-construction group Engergiebaugenossenschaft is characterized by its financing model. Customers who purchase a solar system help with the installation. In addition, they contribute hours of work to the construction of further solar systems. This means that the systems can be built more cheaply.
Themenfelder: Alternative production & consumption, Industry, Ecology, Energy, Education

Beschrieb: In 2010 the doctors from the region founded the association HAN-RT. The family doctors run an emergency practice on the premises of the hospital Thun on weekends from 8.00 to 22.00 o'clock and on weekdays in the evening between 17.00 and 22.00 o'clock. Every day there is a general practitioner who is on duty in the joint emergency practice. The aim is to relieve the workload of the general practitioners and to optimise the emergency work.
